project.name: StudyBattles
project.description: A revision tool where students upload study materials (PPTX, DOCX, YouTube videos), the system generates a topic hierarchy via OpenAI LLM, and they grind through boss encounters to prove understanding. Users answer predetermined question types, get marked against LLM-generated key points, and unlock parent nodes only after defeating all children.
project.completion_criteria: User can upload a document, generate a boss tree, click into bottom nodes, answer questions in predetermined formats, receive marks based on LLM-generated mark schemes, and unlock parent nodes after defeating all children.

phases.title: Project Setup & Infrastructure
phases.deliverable: FastAPI backend running locally, React frontend scaffolded, MongoDB connection established, AWS account configured with S3 bucket for document storage
tasks: Initialize FastAPI project with uvicorn, add basic health check endpoint
Set up MongoDB connection (local for now, can migrate to AWS later)
Initialize React project with Vite or create-react-app, set up routing structure
Create AWS S3 bucket for document uploads, configure IAM user with S3 permissions
Set up environment files (.env) for API keys (OpenAI, AWS credentials, MongoDB URI)
phases.commit_condition: FastAPI server starts on localhost:8000, React frontend loads on localhost:3000, MongoDB connection logs successful, S3 bucket accessible via AWS CLI
phases.example_output: Project skeleton with FastAPI + React running, AWS credentials configured, MongoDB ready

phases.title: Document Upload & Storage Pipeline
phases.deliverable: Endpoint to upload PPTX/DOCX/YouTube URLs, extract text, store in MongoDB with session association
tasks: Create FastAPI endpoint POST /upload to accept file uploads (PPTX, DOCX) and YouTube URLs
Implement text extraction from PPTX and DOCX (using python-pptx and python-docx libraries)
Implement YouTube transcript extraction (using youtube-transcript-api or similar)
Store extracted text in MongoDB with metadata (upload time, file type, user session ID)
Create React component for file upload UI with drag-and-drop support
phases.commit_condition: Can upload a PPTX, DOCX, and YouTube URL via React UI; text extracted and stored in MongoDB; verified in MongoDB shell
phases.example_input: User selects file or pastes YouTube URL
phases.example_output: MongoDB document with extracted text, file metadata, and session ID

phases.title: LLM Topic Hierarchy Generation
phases.deliverable: Endpoint that takes extracted text and generates a topic tree via OpenAI, stores tree structure in MongoDB
tasks: Create FastAPI endpoint POST /generate-tree that accepts document ID
Design prompt for OpenAI to recursively generate topic hierarchy (each topic + prerequisites)
Implement logic to constrain tree depth and branching factor
Store generated tree in MongoDB with phase-lock logic (bottom nodes unlocked, parents locked)
Add validation to ensure tree structure is valid (no cycles, proper parent-child relationships)
phases.commit_condition: API call returns a valid tree structure (JSON with node IDs, titles, parent/child relationships); tree persists in MongoDB
phases.example_output: MongoDB tree document with structure like {id, title, children: [], locked: true/false, prerequisites: []}

phases.title: Boss Question Generation & Mark Scheme
phases.deliverable: For each node in the tree, generate 5 questions (one per predetermined type) with mark schemes via OpenAI
tasks: Create FastAPI endpoint POST /generate-questions that accepts node ID and relevant document text
Design prompts for OpenAI for each question type: Define, Solve, Compare, ELI5, Finish the Answer
Generate 5 questions per node (or fewer if subject doesn't fit all types)
For each question, generate a mark scheme (list of key points) via OpenAI
Store questions + mark schemes in MongoDB, indexed by node ID
phases.commit_condition: For a given node, can retrieve 5 questions with mark schemes; mark schemes are lists of strings (key points)
phases.example_output: MongoDB questions document: {node_id, questions: [{type, text, mark_scheme: []}]}

phases.title: Session & State Management (Backend)
phases.deliverable: Session endpoints to track user progress, unlock status, and attempt history in MongoDB
tasks: Create session model in MongoDB: {session_id, tree_id, node_unlock_status, attempts: {node_id: []}}
Create endpoint POST /session to generate unique session ID and initialize state
Create endpoint GET /session/{session_id} to retrieve current unlock status and progress
Implement unlock logic: when all children beaten, mark parent as unlocked
Create endpoint to track user attempts (answers, marks received, timestamp)
phases.commit_condition: Can create a session, retrieve its state, and verify that parent nodes remain locked until children are beaten
phases.example_input: None (session created on first page load)
phases.example_output: Session document with tree structure and unlock status for each node

phases.title: Frontend: Tree Visualization & Navigation
phases.deliverable: React component to display the boss tree (top-down layout), show node unlock status, allow clicking unlocked nodes
tasks: Design React component to render tree in top-down layout (use Recharts, React Flow, or custom canvas)
Implement visual indicators for locked vs. unlocked nodes
Add click handler to navigate to question screen for unlocked nodes
Fetch tree and unlock status from backend on component mount
Add refresh button to re-fetch state (for testing session persistence)
phases.commit_condition: Tree renders visually, locked nodes appear grayed out, unlocked nodes are clickable, clicking navigates to question page
phases.example_output: Rendered tree component with interactive nodes

phases.title: Frontend: Question Display & Answer Input
phases.deliverable: React component to display a single question and collect user answer in text form
tasks: Create React component for question display (shows question text, type, and node title)
Add textarea for user answer input, with character limit feedback
Add submit button that sends answer to backend
Show loading state while backend evaluates answer
Implement return-to-tree button after answering
phases.commit_condition: Can view a question, type an answer, submit it, and see loading state
phases.example_input: Node ID and question from URL/props

phases.title: Answer Evaluation & Marking Logic
phases.deliverable: FastAPI endpoint to evaluate user answers against mark schemes and return marks
tasks: Create endpoint POST /evaluate that accepts node_id, question_id, and user_answer
Implement Python logic: for each key point in mark scheme, check if key point is 'in' user_answer
Return marks as {marks_received, marks_total, key_points_hit: []} for debugging
Handle edge cases: partial key point matches, case-insensitivity, punctuation tolerance
Store evaluation result in session's attempt history
phases.commit_condition: API returns correct marks for test answers; mark scheme key points are properly checked

phases.title: Frontend: Result Display & Unlock Logic
phases.deliverable: React component to display marks received, show which key points were hit, and visually confirm unlock of parent node if applicable
tasks: Create result component to show marks (e.g., '2/3'), display key points with hit/miss indicators
Show feedback message (e.g., 'You missed: Multiply the coefficient BEFORE...')
If all children beaten, show parent unlock message and animation
Add 'Try again' button to retry the same question (optionally generate new question)
Add 'Back to tree' button to return to tree view
phases.commit_condition: Result component displays correctly; unlock message appears when appropriate; state updates in backend
phases.example_output: User sees marks, key points, and confirmation of progress

phases.title: Session Persistence & Data Retrieval
phases.deliverable: Frontend-side session storage (via session ID in URL or localStorage) ensuring data survives page reload
tasks: Implement session ID generation on first page load (POST /session)
Store session ID in localStorage (or URL as query param)
On page load/reload, fetch session state from backend using stored session ID
Restore tree state, unlock status, and attempt history
Verify that attempting same node twice generates new questions (but same mark scheme initially)
phases.commit_condition: Close and reopen browser; session persists, tree state restored, user can continue from where they left off
phases.example_input: Page reload with existing session ID
phases.example_output: Session state retrieved from MongoDB, UI re-renders with correct state

phases.title: Testing, Bug Fixes & MVP Polish
phases.deliverable: End-to-end testing of core flow, bug fixes, basic error handling, and clean UI
tasks: Test full flow: upload document → generate tree → answer questions → unlock parents
Add error handling for failed document uploads, generation timeouts, API failures
Add loading spinners and user feedback messages throughout
Test session persistence (close/reopen, different tabs, incognito mode if possible)
Fix UI bugs, improve spacing/colors, ensure responsive design for mobile (even if not deployed yet)
Create simple README with instructions to run locally
phases.commit_condition: Full MVP flow works without errors, session persists across reloads, no console errors, UI is clean and responsive
phases.example_output: Fully functional MVP ready for personal testing and iteration
